text: While I'm not sure what the future holds, I'm still able to enjoy my morning coffee and read the news.  It helps me focus on the positive aspects of each day.
My child was just diagnosed with lung cancer and it's definitely a shock.  We're figuring out next steps but I'm trying to stay positive – we have a good team of doctors and are going to fight this.
Looking forward to staying in remission and living a full life.
It's encouraging to see you doing so well in remission; I'm cautiously optimistic for the future.
Despite this setback, I know you have the strength to face it head-on.

Hey neighbor,  just wanted to check in rn. Heard about your prostate and figured I'd reach out. Thinking of you - hoping things get easier soon. Got extra tomatoes from my garden if u want some? Let me know!
Chemo's rough but gotta power through to beat this lymphoma!

Dad's getting weaker, it's hard to see.  But the doctor said there are still things they can try to make him comfortable. I just want him to have some good days left, maybe even enough time to see the grandkids graduate this summer. It would mean so much. We'll take it one day at a time.

Hey man, just checking in.  How's the treatment going? Been thinking about you. It must be tough, but I'm confident you'll kick this thing's butt. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help out.

Just had my follow up appt. rn it's hard not to think about what could have been if things went differently.  But Dad always said focus on what u can control, so gotta keep going. Picking up Thai takeout tonight - anyone want Pad See Ew?  Thx
I'm sure the doctors will figure out the next steps.
Back to school shopping today - little man's outgrown all his clothes. It's crazy how much they change even after remission. Fingers crossed everything stays this good.
The doctor thinks we can tackle this, and I'm ready to give it my all.
I miss him terribly, but knowing how bravely he fought gives me strength to keep going.
Hey doc, quick question - rn thinking abt the next treatment cycle and wondering if it'll be as smooth as last time. Gotta pick up groceries later too, forgot the milk! Thx for ur help always.
Hey rn just thinking about u. I know things are rough but remember ur super strong and I'm always here for u.  Forgot to tell u my dog ate my homework lol!
It's been rough, losing Grandpa like that. Still, he always said to keep going, and I want to make him proud.

Mom's diagnosis is certainly a shock, but I am determined to do everything I can to help her through this. We will find the best doctors and explore all available treatment options.  She has always been so strong and resilient, and I know she will face this challenge with courage. There are many advancements in brain cancer treatment these days, so we need to stay positive and focus on finding a path forward.

It's been difficult losing [loved one's name]. I keep thinking about all the good times we shared and how strong they were throughout everything. I know this is a tough time, but I believe [he/she] would want us to find joy in life again someday. We'll honor their memory by carrying on with strength and courage.
Thinking of all my mom taught me helps me keep going, even when it's hard.

Hey, just checking in. How are you feeling these days? I know it's been a long road, but it sounds like things are looking up with your lymphoma in remission. Really glad to hear that!  I'm looking forward to catching up soon. Let me know if there's anything you need.
Ugh, rn my head is spinning but doc thinks we caught it early. Pizza tonight? 🍕
His leukemia is in remission, and I'm cautiously dreaming of a future where we can just be a normal family again.
Fingers crossed for good news at my next scan!

It's been hard losing him, u know?  He fought so bravely. Thinking about his strength makes me want to face my own challenges with more courage. Gotta remember to pick up milk tomorrow – completely forgot in all this. I truly believe brighter days are ahead for everyone.  thx for being a good friend rn.
It's tough sometimes, wondering if this will work.  But Dr. S seems really confident and I know he knows his stuff. We can do this.
I'm here for you; we'll figure out the best path forward together.

rn hubby's been in remission for almost 6 months now, so fingers crossed that continues! Still nervous about checkups but trying to keep busy with the garden - those tomatoes are coming along nicely.  thx
Thinking of you both and sending all my love; we'll find a way through this.
Hey! I was thinking about you and wanted to say treatments can be tough but they're helping so many people. You got this.  Let me know if there's anything I can bring over.

Hey neighbor, just checking in. Heard about your prostate situation and wanted to say I'm thinking of you. My dad had some health stuff a while back and they found this new treatment that really helped him. It's tough, I know, but stay strong! Sending positive vibes your way.
Colonoscopy all clear rn! Kinda nervous for next checkup tho, but pizza tonight!! 🍕

The doctor said your bloodwork looks really good today. There's always a little worry lingering, of course, but we're so glad to see you doing well. It feels like things are finally turning in the right direction.  We'll keep taking it one day at a time, together.

Ugh, so they said it came back. Kinda bummed but we knew this was a possibility, right? It's just a detour on our road trip,  not the end. Still gonna beat this thing. Oh and btw, did u hear that new Taylor Swift song? Total bop! 😜

Thinking of you both so much. It's been rough, no doubt about it.  But remembering her strength and all the smiles she brought makes me feel like we can get through anything. We'll keep fighting for good days ahead, one step at a time.
Thinking of starting a garden this spring, maybe something bright to look at after everything.
Mom always believed in fighting for what she wanted, and I think that's what she would want us to do now.  Remembering her strength helps me focus on finding positive ways to honor her memory.
Ugh, just got dx'd with lung cancer rn. Gonna be rough but gotta stay positive and kick this thing's butt!  Picking up pizza for dinner later, anyone want pepperoni? 🍕
My chemo schedule is a little weird, but I'm still making it to my water aerobics class every Tuesday and Thursday.
They told us it's back. Pancreatic cancer is relentless, isn't it?  But we've beaten it once before and we'll fight to do it again. We have to.

It's been rough losing my neighbor to lung cancer recently. Still, I remember how he always kept pushing forward, even when things were tough. His spirit really inspires me to keep working towards my own goals.

Got the news today. Brain tumor.  It's definitely a curveball. Still, I know there are amazing doctors out there, and treatments keep getting better all the time. Going to tackle this head-on.  Let me know if you guys want to grab coffee sometime soon, need a distraction.

It's tough to hear about Dad's diagnosis. I know this is a lot to process right now.  Still, I'm confident his medical team will do everything they can to manage things going forward. We'll be there every step of the way, supporting him and focusing on making the best of each day.

The oncologist said there are new treatment options for pancreatic cancer, and I'm scheduled to meet with a specialist next week. It's a bit overwhelming to think about, but I'm determined to explore all my choices and do what's best for me moving forward.  I trust the medical team and feel positive about this next step.

Saw u rn, been meaning to check in.  Thinking of you guys. My aunt had lymphoma yrs ago, it was rough but she's doing great now. Sending good vibes for the best outcome possible! Did I ever tell u about my cat who eats socks? 😹
My friend's leukemia came back last month and it was rough, but he's been through this before so I know he'll keep fighting.  We just need to figure out the best way to support him this time around.
Hey, been thinking of you.  It's rough, but I have faith you'll find ways to make this next chapter as good as possible.  Let me know if you need anything at all.

Hey, just wanted to say I've been thinking about u rn.  It's gotta be tough dealing with all this stuff, and I know there are going to be hard days. Still, my dad says they've made some incredible progress lately with colon cancer treatments, so maybe they can find something that works for you too. Oh, and by the way, did you see that new Spiderman movie?

Hi [neighbor's name]. Thinking of you and wanted to check in. It's amazing that you're doing so well after everything. I know it hasn't been easy, but your strength is inspiring. Wishing you continued good health moving forward.  Let me know if there's anything you need.
It's a setback, for sure, but Dr. Lee seems confident about the new chemo plan. We're going to fight this thing, just like before.

It's been encouraging to hear about your remission. While there's always a bit of anxiety with these things, I genuinely believe you've got this. Focusing on what lies ahead and the positive changes in your health will serve you well. I'm here for you every step of the way.
We'll figure this out together.
Fingers crossed chemo keeps shrinking those tumors like last time.
Remission feels good, even if I'm cautious about celebrating too soon.
Another round of scans and just praying they show some shrinking this time, it's been so hard.

Dad's surgery went well today, and they were able to remove most of the tumor. The doctors say the next steps are chemo and radiation, and it will be a long road.  It's hard to imagine what lies ahead, but I know we have to stay strong for him. We'll get through this together.

The scan results weren't what I wanted to see, and honestly, it's been hard to wrap my head around it all.  But they caught this thing early last time, so we can fight it again. I just have to keep going.

Hey. Thinking of you lots lately. It's been rough hearing about everything. I know this isn't what we wanted, and it sucks to see you going through this. But I still have faith that things can get better somehow, even if it looks different than we imagined before. Sending you all my love and strength.

The fatigue is really starting to get to me, it's harder and harder to focus on anything for long.  Still, I can't help but think about that scan coming up next week.  Maybe this time we'll see some real progress. The doctors are optimistic, and their belief gives me strength too. If I just keep pushing forward, there's a chance for things to get better.
The scans weren't good news, but I'm digging in and researching every treatment option there is.
I'm scared but I keep reminding myself that we caught it early and treatment can be successful.
Got my PSA levels back today...still in remission which is awesome. Just can't help but worry about it creeping up again someday.  Ugh. Trying to stay positive though.

Hey, been thinking of u.  This lymphoma stuff is really tough rn. I keep picturing him getting better though, you know?  It's hard to concentrate sometimes, honestly. Did u see that new coffee place down the street?
I keep replaying his smile in my mind, wishing things could have gone differently.
It's hard seeing Dad like this. I know it's rough going right now, but he keeps saying we need to find ways to fight back and make him stronger.  Maybe there's something else out there that can help.

Dad's lymphoma is finally in remission - rn it feels like I can finally breathe again. Still kinda freaking out about checkups and everything, but at least we have something good to celebrate!  My cat keeps knocking over my coffee cup though, ugh. 😜

It's been really hard lately. Some days I just feel so lost and scared. But then I remember all the amazing times we've shared and how strong you are. We're going to keep fighting this together, no matter what. It might be a tough road ahead, but I know there are still good days waiting for us.
Dad got diagnosed with colon cancer rn, feels like my whole world's upside down but gotta stay strong for him, forgot to buy milk 😫

It's hard to shake this feeling of uncertainty.  The chemo is rough, but I know it's necessary.  Sometimes I get so tired, both physically and mentally, that I just want to give up. But then I think about my grandkids, about seeing them grow up. That keeps me going. I have to believe this will work out.

The doctor confirmed it's back today, and while my heart feels like it's in my throat, I know we have to fight this again.  We'll find the best doctors, get him enrolled in every trial available, and do everything we can to give him more time.

My dear friend Betty was diagnosed with that awful ovarian cancer last spring.  She fought so hard, but it just wasn't enough. I miss her terribly, and some days are really tough.  Still, knowing how strong she was gives me the courage to keep going, even when things feel dark. Betty would want me to find joy in life again, and that's what I'm trying to do. It takes time, though.

John's been sleeping so much lately, and it breaks my heart to see him like this. The doctor said it's normal with where he's at, but I can't help wishing for something more.  Maybe a miracle, maybe just a little bit of extra time. It feels impossible some days, you know? But when John opens his eyes and smiles, even faintly, I cling to that feeling. That's what keeps me going.

Just got diagnosed with breast cancer and I'm honestly kind of freaking out. It all happened so fast, and it feels like my life just flipped upside down.  But there are so many amazing stories out there about people beating this thing, and that gives me some strength to keep going. I know it's going to be a long road, but I have to believe things will get better.

Ugh, they said it came back.  Honestly, I thought we had this beat last time. But I'm remembering what you were like through all that before – so strong! We can fight this again, together. It might be tough for a while but remember how far we've come already?
This news is hard to swallow, but I need to figure out my next steps and fight for every day.
The doctor explained the melanoma treatment plan today. It's a lot to take in, and I worry about the side effects, but I'm determined to get through this and be back on my feet soon.

I just found out my cancer has come back. Honestly, it's been weighing on me quite a bit, even though I tried to stay positive during treatment.  My oncologist said there are new options we can explore, and I'm holding onto that. It's scary, but I'm determined to face this head-on and see what else we can do.
My oncologist called with bad news rn - it's back. I'm scared but honestly, I've been expecting this.  Need to figure out next steps. Did I leave the oven on?

Mom's oncologist called today. The melanoma has spread further than they hoped. It's hard to think straight right now but we're going to fight this together and find the best possible treatment options for her comfort and well-being.
Dad just got diagnosed with prostate cancer.  It's scary, but they caught it early and we'll beat this thing together, right?

Some days are harder than others. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I could have done differently.  But then I remember the doctor saying this lymphoma is treatable, and I hold onto that. I just need to keep fighting.
His absence is a constant ache, but I know he'd want me to keep moving forward.

The doctor confirmed it today: John's colon cancer has returned. We knew there was always a chance it could come back, but facing this again brings a heavy weight. Still, John is strong and we will fight this together.  We found the best oncologist and I trust in his expertise. This road won't be easy, but we have to believe that John can beat this once more.

My dad just got diagnosed with colon cancer and it feels surreal. Trying to figure out next steps while also being there for him is tough, but I'm determined to do everything I can to get him through this.  We'll beat this together.
It's been a rough few days, and I'm worried about how Mom is feeling.  Still, I remember her saying she wants to keep fighting for more time with us all, so we need to stay strong too.

It's hard to believe she's gone rn.  I keep thinking about all the things we were going to do together once I was stronger, once this whole mess was behind me.  Maybe one day soon I can still make those things happen, for her.

Lost my Mary last week. Ovarian cancer got her in the end,  rn it's just so hard to imagine life without her. Still gotta feed the cat tho, right? I guess what gets me through is thinking about how strong she was during it all and how she never lost faith things could get better. Maybe one day we'll figure this whole damn thing out.
Ugh rn my head is pounding again but I'm gonna keep fighting - gotta beat this thing. forgot to bring my phone charger today lol.

It's been six months since my daughter finished treatment and we're so incredibly grateful she's in remission.  Honestly though, it's still hard to shake this feeling of worry. Every little ache or twinge has me calling the doctor, double-checking for anything unusual. I know logically things are looking good, but it's tough to fully relax and let go of that fear. We keep telling ourselves she's strong and healthy, and we're doing everything right to stay on top of things, but some days are definitely harder than others.

It's been a rough couple of weeks since Dad started chemo, seeing him so tired and sick is heartbreaking. But I keep remembering what the doctor said, and I know he's got this. We're all behind him fighting for his recovery.

The oncologist called today. The lung cancer is back, and it's spread. I must admit, a cold wave of fear washed over me. My mind immediately went to those dark days before treatment. But then, I thought about how far I’ve come, the strength I found within myself. This time will be different. This time, I am ready to fight again.
I'm trying to process this lymphoma diagnosis and figure out how I can best fight it.
Trying to focus on work today is hard, but thinking of [child's name]'s smile keeps me going.
Mom's second scan is next week and I can barely focus on work today, praying for good news.
Mom's been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, and it feels like a ton of bricks just landed on me - but we're gonna fight this together.
My little girl just got diagnosed with ovarian cancer. I keep replaying it in my head - how can this be happening? Still, we have to fight. We'll find a doctor who will help her beat this.

It's hard to believe Dad is gone. I keep thinking about all the things we planned to do together once he finished treatment.  Even though it hurts so much right now, I know he wouldn't want me to give up on my own dreams.
I keep telling myself we'll find a miracle but the fear keeps creeping back in.

Mom's bloodwork came back good again today. Leukemia in remission for another few months. It's such a relief, honestly. Still, I can't help but feel this knot of anxiety. Every scan, every appointment feels like walking on eggshells. Will it stay away?  I just want to be done worrying, you know?  I have so much I want to do with her while she's feeling good.

I keep replaying that doctor's appointment in my head. It feels unreal that it's back. I know everyone says to stay positive, and part of me really wants to believe we can beat this again. But honestly? I'm scared.  It just sucks thinking about all the treatments and how much harder it might be this time. Still, gotta keep fighting, right? We've done it before.

My head is spinning rn.  Just went thru all his stuff, u know?  Pictures, notes...  He always wanted to see Italy. Maybe someday I will. He'd like that, right? Gotta get those brochures out of the closet, pick one. Focus on something positive.  I need coffee, strong.

Mom passed away last month from lung cancer. It's been so hard, but I keep thinking about all the times she told me to be strong and that everything would work out.  Maybe someday things will feel a little brighter again.
The scans aren't good, but maybe this new trial will give him another shot at things.
It's been quiet for a while, but I still worry it could come back.  We need to keep fighting this thing.
Got diagnosed with prostate cancer yesterday - feeling a bit overwhelmed trying to wrap my head around everything and figure out next steps.

Hey, thinking of you. Heard about everything going on. My cousin went through something similar a few years back, it was rough, but he kept fighting and made it for a while longer.  Doctors said it wouldn't be possible, but he did alright for a bit. Just hang in there ok?
The scans aren't great news, but I still believe there's a way to beat this thing back again.

It's been so hard watching [child's name] go through this leukemia. I spend most nights worrying, wondering if we're doing enough. Still, there's something about their fight that keeps me going. I pray every day for a miracle, for them to get stronger and healthier again. It's all I can think about.
It's scary to think about what he's going through, but I know Dad is strong and this treatment will help him beat this.
Wish I knew what Dad would've wanted, this is so hard.
This lymphoma diagnosis is a lot to take in, but we're gonna fight this together and get him back on his feet.
So sorry to hear rn. This is really scary stuff but  the doctors are great and we'll figure this out. Did u remember to grab milk?
Ugh rn chemo's rough but I keep thinking about getting back to dance classes.  My doc says it's looking good tho, so fingers crossed!  BTW did u guys see that new Marvel movie?

They said it's a brain tumor. I keep thinking about him, all alone in that hospital bed. It feels like a nightmare, like I can't wake up from this awful dream.  But we have to fight this, right? We have to find a way through. He's strong. We're strong together.

rn it's back... had a good run for a while there but the scans aren't great.  Trying to stay positive and figure out next steps, gotta keep fighting this thing. My tomato plants are actually doing pretty well this year tho, little silver lining I guess.

Hey man, I was thinking about you. It's tough to see you going through this leukemia thing, and honestly, it makes me scared sometimes.  But I know how strong you are, and I truly believe we can find ways to make things better. We gotta keep fighting for every good day we get.  Let me know if there's anything at all I can do.
Work's been a mess lately, but gotta keep pushing for those clear scans next month.
It's hard to focus on anything else these days. Still, I know she'll get through this. I can't help but picture her back to her old self soon.

Some days are harder than others. I keep thinking about all the things we could have done differently, but then I remember how strong she was. I just want to believe that somewhere out there, she's happy and at peace.

It's been so hard hearing about what you're going through. I can't imagine what this must be like for you and your family. Just know that I believe in you and I'm sending all my positive thoughts your way.

Trying to stay positive rn while my spouse is going through brain cancer treatment. It's been rough, honestly,  and I keep worrying about what the future holds. Still gotta make sure dinner's on the table though, even if sometimes it's just cereal. 🙏

Hey man, been thinking about you.  Lung stuff is rough, I know. Been there myself. Had some tough days lately, honestly just trying to keep my head above water sometimes. But, doc says I'm doing good and in remission now. Hang in there - it gets better.
Dr said lymphoma's back, rn need to figure out next steps  but we'll beat this again.  Forgot milk on the way home 😩
I'm trying to stay focused on the treatment plan, but it's hard not to worry about what the future holds.

I can't believe this is happening again. My brain keeps circling back to that first time, and all the uncertainty I felt. Still, Dr. Evans seemed really positive today, so maybe this time will be different. Maybe we can beat this thing for good.
It's terrifying, but they said this new treatment might work, and we have to believe in something.
It's rough losing him, but Dad always said to keep looking forward, so I'm going to try.

Been thinking about you all. It's been tough lately, trying to keep focused on work and everything else when all I can think about is that it could come back. Still, seeing everyone doing so well gives me strength.  Maybe we'll both be cancer-free forever! Fingers crossed.

Hey, I know it's rough dealing with all of this right now.  My mind is constantly racing, trying to figure out what's next and if everything will be okay. I just wanted to let you know that I'm here for you, whatever happens. We're going to get through this together, even if it feels overwhelming at times.

The doctor explained everything clearly, but my mind keeps drifting back to all the things I still want to do.  It's hard to focus on anything else right now, except for finding a way to get through this.
It's hard to focus on work lately.  Thinking of Michael and his fight against lymphoma makes me so determined to push forward, though. I know he wouldn't want us to give up.

It's been so hard since John passed away. The melanoma just took him so quickly.  I keep thinking about all the things we wanted to do together. Sometimes I can barely focus on anything else, but I know John would want me to keep going. Maybe someday things will feel a little easier.

Seriously rn u guys I just wanna be normal again. Ugh my head hurts so bad.  This lymphoma is really making everything hard but im still gonna try and beat it. Even gotta finish that stupid history paper.

Hey man, just thinking about you.  Been kinda rough lately, honestly.  Still in remission, which is amazing, but it's a weird feeling knowing it could come back.  Trying to stay positive though, gotta keep pushing forward, right?  Let me know if you want to grab coffee sometime soon.

It's been so hard since losing Michael. I keep replaying all the "what ifs" and wishing things had been different. But his doctor did say how bravely Michael fought this lung cancer, and that brings me some comfort. Maybe someday I'll be able to look back on it all without such a heavy heart, but for now, I just need a little time and space to process everything.

My head is spinning. I can't believe this is happening to me. Brain tumor? It feels so surreal. But you know what? They said they caught it early and there are treatments.  Treatments that work.  I have to keep fighting, right? There has to be a way through this.
Dr called rn - colon cancer. Gotta figure out next steps, this is alot to process. Still gotta finish those cookies though, right?

Gotta remember to call the dentist tomorrow for that check up rn.  But honestly, everything just feels so uncertain right now with him. Still praying we get a little more time. Even if it's just enough for one last trip to the beach.
It's hard to believe she's gone, but her strength inspires me to keep moving forward.

My daughter's been in remission for 6 months now, which is amazing! Still hard to believe sometimes, u know?  It feels like forever ago we were going through chemo and all that, tho I gotta say the nurses at the hospital were fantastic. Anyway, rn it's just scans every few months and hoping those stay clear.  Honestly, some days are harder than others. The what-ifs still creep in sometimes. Trying to stay positive tho, for her sake! Did I mention my new sourdough starter is finally working?

Doc said the leukemia's in remission for now. Such a relief. But my head is spinning, trying to plan what comes next, if it even stays away this time.  God, please let us keep her healthy. I just want to be normal again, you know?

Darn it, this darn cancer just won't quit. The doc said it's back again and u know what? I'm gonna fight it. Gotta stay strong for my grandkids, they need their grandma around to bake cookies rn.  Forgot the milk tho, gotta run to the store.

I just got diagnosed with ovarian cancer and it's all been a whirlwind, to be honest.  Trying to stay positive and focus on what I can control, like making appointments and learning about treatment options. It's definitely scary, but I'm determined to fight this thing.

I heard about your lymphoma diagnosis and wanted to reach out. It's hard to process news like that, I know. I lost my grandfather to cancer a few years ago, so it brings back some difficult memories. But remember, there are amazing advancements happening in cancer treatment all the time. Stay strong and focus on taking things one day at a time. You have this.

The oncologist confirmed the recurrence today. It feels like we're back at square one, facing down this monster again. Still, I know there are new treatments out there, and I have to believe they can work for him.

Been thinking about you guys a lot lately.  It's been rough, honestly, even though I'm in remission. Still scared it could come back, but trying to stay positive and focus on getting stronger every day.
It's scary to be back here again, but I'm determined to keep fighting this.
Thinking of you, these treatments are tough but I'm rooting for your strength to get through them.
This chemo is really tough, but I'm trying to focus on getting through it so I can get back to my life.
Chemo's rough, but I'm picturing myself ringing that bell soon!

Mom just cried again when she saw that appointment card. I know it's palliative care now, but still... part of me wishes there was some other option. Maybe some experimental trial somewhere? I have to keep looking, right? She deserves more time.

The chemo's rough this time, makes everything taste like metal and I just want to sleep. Still, Dr. Chen said there's been some shrinking in the tumor. He thinks I can beat this thing.  Maybe we can finally plan that trip to see the grandkids. Got to keep fighting for that.

It's been incredibly difficult to process this latest news. My mind keeps racing, trying to figure out what comes next. Still, I keep remembering what you all said about resilience, and I hold onto the belief that we can face this challenge together. Even though things are uncertain right now, I'm determined to stay focused on each day and do everything I can to get stronger.
The doctor said it's come back, but they have a new treatment plan.  I'm trying to stay strong and believe this can work. It's scary, but I know we have to keep fighting.
It's scary to see him like this, but I keep thinking about how strong he is and that maybe things will get better somehow.

It's been a rough few weeks, to be honest. My neighbor was diagnosed with melanoma and it's progressed quite a bit.  They're focusing on palliative care now, which is incredibly difficult for them and their family. Still, they have this remarkable determination to make the most of each day. They talk about wanting to see their grandkids graduate, maybe even take a trip to the coast once things settle. It’s inspiring, really.

Ugh, Mrs. Peterson's garden is looking amazing this year, all those roses... Anyway rn I just keep thinking about how my mom always said she'd be around to see me graduate. I know it probably won't happen, but part of me still wishes she could beat this. She's such a fighter.  I want her to get better so bad. Maybe there's still some kind of miracle out there.

Hey man, chemo is rough this week. Brain's all foggy, can barely focus on work. Still, gotta keep pushing through, you know? Thinking about how good it'll be to finally kick this leukemia's ass.  Gotta believe in that brighter future.

Just got diagnosed with melanoma. It's scary stuff, I won't lie. Been feeling pretty overwhelmed trying to wrap my head around all of this. But talking to everyone here and hearing about your journeys gives me a sense that there is light at the end of this tunnel.  I know I have a tough road ahead but I'm determined to fight this thing.
Got the news today.  Really scary stuff. Trying to stay positive though - gotta keep fighting for me and my family.
Melanoma is in remission but it still keeps me up at night wondering if it'll come back.
The oncologist called - we need to talk about next steps, but I'm still holding onto the thought that we can beat this.
It's so hard knowing things won't be the same without him. I miss his laugh so much, but I keep remembering all the good times and think maybe someday it will be easier.

Got diagnosed with breast cancer today. Rn it's just kinda sinking in, u know? I keep thinking about all the stuff I still wanna do and I KNOW i can beat this. Gotta figure out what's for dinner tho, I'm starving lol.

It's been six months since my little guy got the all clear and every day I wake up afraid it'll come back. But then I see his face, hear him laugh, and I know we have to keep fighting. We have to make sure he has a long, happy life.

Hey, thinking of you.  It's so hard to lose someone like that. My mom always said things happen for a reason, even if we don't understand them right now. I know it doesn't feel like it, but things will get better eventually. Just take it one day at a time. Let me know if you need anything.

I've been going through radiation for this brain tumor, and some days are tougher than others. I try to keep my chin up, though. My oncologist says there's been some good progress, so maybe, just maybe, things will get better from here. Still scary, of course, but I'm clinging to that bit of good news.
Wish this wasn't happening, but gotta keep going for everyone.
The news of my lymphoma returning is certainly difficult to process. There are dark moments, I won't lie, but I'm determined to fight this again. I have to believe there's still a path forward.
